ddls:
CREATE TABLE "doctors" (
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each doctor */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the doctor */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the doctor's practice */,
  "experience_years" INTEGER /* Years of experience in dermatology */,
  "consultation_fee" TEXT /* Consultation fee charged by the doctor */,
  "profile_link" TEXT /* Link to the doctor's profile for more information */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography or detailed description of the doctor */,
  "photo" TEXT /* Photo of the doctor */,
  "qualification" TEXT /* Qualification of the doctor */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Specialization of the doctor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doctor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clinics" (
  "clinic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clinic */,
  "clinic_name" TEXT /* Name of the clinic */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the clinic */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the clinic */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the clinic for more information */,
  "directions" TEXT /* Link for directions to the clinic */,
  "map_coordinates" TEXT /* Map coordinates for clinic location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("clinic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "doctor_clinic" (
  "doctor_clinic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for doctor-clinic association */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor */,
  "clinic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clinic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doctor_clinic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "appointments" (
  "appointment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each appointment */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor for the appointment */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient booking the appointment */,
  "appointment_date" TEXT /* Date of the appointment */,
  "appointment_time" TEXT /* Time of the appointment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the appointment (e.g., scheduled, completed, canceled) */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service offered by the doctor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("appointment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_appointments_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_appointments_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_appointments_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the patient */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the patient */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor providing the service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service offered */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the service */,
  "service_fee" TEXT /* Fee charged for the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "availability" (
  "availability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each availability record */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor whose availability is being recorded */,
  "day_of_week" TEXT /* Day of the week for the availability */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the availability */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the availability */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("availability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_availability_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient associated with the history */,
  "appointment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appointment related to the visit */,
  "visit_date" TEXT /* Date of the visit */,
  "visit_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments regarding the visit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_history_appointment_id FOREIGN KEY ("appointment_id") REFERENCES appointments ("appointment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_history_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who receives the notification */,
  "appointment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appointment related to the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., appointment confirmation, reminder) */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_appointment_id FOREIGN KEY ("appointment_id") REFERENCES appointments ("appointment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, doctor, patient) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor being reviewed */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient leaving the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the patient (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was left */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "appointment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appointment */,
  "appointment_date" TEXT /* Date of the appointment */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service offered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the appointment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_appointment_id FOREIGN KEY ("appointment_id") REFERENCES appointments ("appointment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)
